questionText: Você quer dizer a um amigo próximo que ele pode contar com você em qualquer situação difícil. Qual expressão em inglês transmite esse apoio incondicional de forma mais natural e idiomática?
correctAnswer: I've got your back, no matter what.
options: I've got your back, no matter what.
I will support you when it is necessary.
You can count on me if you need help.
I am always here for assistance.

questionText: Você deseja parabenizar um casal pelo nascimento do primeiro filho de forma calorosa e idiomática em inglês. Qual expressão é a mais natural?
correctAnswer: Congratulations on the new arrival! You must be over the moon.
options: Congratulations on the new arrival! You must be over the moon.
Happy to know that your baby was born. You are very happy.
Congratulations for the baby. It is a very good news.
I congratulate you both for having a new son or daughter.

questionText: Sua irmã está passando por um divórcio difícil e você quer demonstrar empatia sem minimizar a dor dela. Qual frase em inglês é a mais adequada para essa situação?
correctAnswer: I can only imagine how hard this must be for you. I'm here whenever you need to talk.
options: I can only imagine how hard this must be for you. I'm here whenever you need to talk.
Divorces are difficult, but you will feel better soon.
Don't worry so much. Many people go through divorces.
You should be strong and move on with your life.

questionText: Você quer cumprimentar seus sogros pela primeira vez e causar uma boa impressão. Qual expressão em inglês é a mais adequada e respeitosa?
correctAnswer: It's a pleasure to finally meet you. I've heard so much about you.
options: It's a pleasure to finally meet you. I've heard so much about you.
Hello. Nice to see you today.
Good day to you both. How are you doing?
Hi there! I am very happy to be here.

questionText: Você quer pedir desculpas ao seu parceiro por ter dito algo rude durante uma discussão. Qual expressão em inglês é a mais sincera e direta?
correctAnswer: I'm truly sorry for what I said. I was out of line and I deeply regret it.
options: I'm truly sorry for what I said. I was out of line and I deeply regret it.
I apologize for the things I said during our fight.
Sorry about that. I didn't want to say those words.
I am sorry if my words offended you in some way.

questionText: Você quer dizer ao seu filho adolescente que está orgulhoso dele após uma conquista importante. Qual expressão é a mais natural e afetiva em inglês?
correctAnswer: I couldn't be prouder of you. You've worked so hard for this.
options: I couldn't be prouder of you. You've worked so hard for this.
I am proud because you did a good job.
You did very well and I feel happy for you.
Good work. I am satisfied with your performance.

questionText: Você quer expressar que sua família sempre foi muito unida, mesmo nos momentos difíceis. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês transmite melhor essa ideia?
correctAnswer: We've always stuck together through thick and thin.
options: We've always stuck together through thick and thin.
Our family has always been together in good and bad times.
We always support each other when things are difficult.
My family members help each other in all situations.

questionText: Você quer convidar um parente distante para se aproximar e manter mais contato. Qual frase em inglês é a mais natural para essa situação?
correctAnswer: We should catch up sometime — it's been far too long!
options: We should catch up sometime — it's been far too long!
Let's meet each other because we haven't talked for a long time.
We need to have contact because we are distant relatives.
I think we should see each other more often in the future.

questionText: Você quer consolar um amigo cujo pai acabou de falecer. Qual expressão de condolências em inglês é a mais empática e culturalmente adequada?
correctAnswer: I'm so sorry for your loss. Please know that I'm here for you.
options: I'm so sorry for your loss. Please know that I'm here for you.
I am sad because your father died. It must be very painful.
Sorry to hear that your father is no longer alive. That is sad.
I feel sorry for the death of your father. Be strong.

questionText: Você quer descrever um relacionamento em que os parceiros têm personalidades opostas mas se complementam muito bem. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês é a mais apropriada?
correctAnswer: They're complete opposites, but they complement each other perfectly — a real case of opposites attract.
options: They're complete opposites, but they complement each other perfectly — a real case of opposites attract.
They are very different people but they have a good relationship.
Although they don't have the same personality, they like each other.
They are not similar but they manage to get along well together.

questionText: Você quer dizer que um casal parece muito feliz e apaixonado juntos. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: They're head over heels for each other.
options: They're head over heels for each other.
They love each other very strongly and with passion.
That couple is very much in love and happy together.
They seem to have a very deep and romantic relationship.

questionText: Você quer dizer que seus pais são muito generosos e sempre fizeram sacrifícios por você. Qual frase em inglês expressa isso de forma mais idiomática?
correctAnswer: My parents have always gone above and beyond for me.
options: My parents have always gone above and beyond for me.
My parents have always made many sacrifices for my benefit.
My parents are generous people and they help me a lot.
My parents always do more than what is required for me.

questionText: Você quer dizer que não se dá bem com um familiar específico e evita contato com ele. Qual expressão em inglês é a mais natural para essa situação?
correctAnswer: We just don't see eye to eye, so I tend to keep my distance.
options: We just don't see eye to eye, so I tend to keep my distance.
I don't have a good relationship with that person so I avoid them.
We are not compatible so I prefer not to be near that family member.
I don't like to be with that relative because we always disagree.

questionText: Você quer descrever que seu irmão se parece muito com o pai em termos de comportamento e aparência. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: He's the spitting image of our father, and he acts just like him too.
options: He's the spitting image of our father, and he acts just like him too.
My brother looks exactly like our father and has the same behaviors.
My brother is very similar to dad in terms of appearance and personality.
He resembles our father both physically and in terms of character.

questionText: Você quer parabenizar um casal pelo aniversário de casamento de forma calorosa e idiomática. Qual frase em inglês é a mais natural?
correctAnswer: Happy anniversary! Here's to many more wonderful years together.
options: Happy anniversary! Here's to many more wonderful years together.
Congratulations on your wedding anniversary. I hope you continue together.
Happy anniversary. I wish you good luck in your married life.
Good day on your anniversary. I hope you have more years of marriage.

questionText: Você quer expressar que sente muito a falta de um familiar que mora longe. Qual frase em inglês expressa esse sentimento de saudade de forma mais natural?
correctAnswer: I miss you terribly — the house just isn't the same without you.
options: I miss you terribly — the house just isn't the same without you.
I feel your absence a lot and the house is different without you.
I am missing you because you are not at home with us anymore.
Without you here, I feel very sad and I miss your presence.

questionText: Você quer descrever uma família em que todos se envolvem demais nos assuntos uns dos outros. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês captura melhor esse comportamento?
correctAnswer: They're an incredibly close-knit family, though they do tend to meddle in each other's affairs.
options: They're an incredibly close-knit family, though they do tend to meddle in each other's affairs.
That family is very united but they also interfere in each other's lives.
They have a tight family where everyone participates in family problems.
That family is close but the members always mix into each other's business.

questionText: Você está tentando mediar um conflito entre dois irmãos e quer pedir que eles coloquem de lado as diferenças por um momento. Qual frase em inglês é a mais adequada para essa mediação?
correctAnswer: Could you both try to set aside your differences and hear each other out?
options: Could you both try to set aside your differences and hear each other out?
Please stop fighting and listen to what the other person has to say.
I ask that you both put away your problems and talk to each other.
Can you two ignore your disagreements and have a calm conversation?

questionText: Seu cunhado constantemente se intromete na sua vida conjugal com conselhos não solicitados. Você quer estabelecer um limite de forma assertiva mas respeitosa. Qual frase em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: I appreciate your concern, but my partner and I need to work this out on our own.
options: I appreciate your concern, but my partner and I need to work this out on our own.
Please don't give us advice because we don't want your opinion.
Stop interfering in our relationship because it is our private matter.
We don't need your help with our marriage, so please stay away.

questionText: Você quer descrever que uma amizade de infância cresceu e se transformou em um amor romântico ao longo dos anos. Qual expressão em inglês captura esse desenvolvimento de forma mais elegante?
correctAnswer: What started as a childhood friendship gradually blossomed into something much deeper.
options: What started as a childhood friendship gradually blossomed into something much deeper.
They were friends when they were children and later they became a couple.
Their friendship from childhood changed slowly into a romantic relationship.
After being friends for many years, they eventually started to love each other.

questionText: Você quer dizer que apesar das brigas frequentes, dois irmãos têm um vínculo inquebrável. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês captura melhor essa ideia?
correctAnswer: They bicker constantly, but when push comes to shove, they'd do anything for each other.
options: They bicker constantly, but when push comes to shove, they'd do anything for each other.
Although they argue a lot, they have a very strong relationship between them.
They fight frequently but in the end they always support each other.
Despite their many disagreements, those siblings care about each other deeply.

questionText: Você quer contar que sua avó foi quem realmente te criou, já que seus pais trabalhavam muito. Qual frase em inglês expressa isso de forma mais natural e precisa?
correctAnswer: My grandmother essentially raised me — she was more of a parent to me than my own parents.
options: My grandmother essentially raised me — she was more of a parent to me than my own parents.
My grandmother took care of me because my parents were always working.
It was my grandmother who helped me grow up while my parents worked.
My parents worked a lot so my grandmother was responsible for raising me.

questionText: Você quer expressar que um relacionamento acabou de forma gradual e sem um evento específico que o causou. Qual frase idiomática em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: We didn't have a dramatic falling-out; we just gradually drifted apart.
options: We didn't have a dramatic falling-out; we just gradually drifted apart.
Our relationship finished slowly and there was no specific reason for it.
We ended our relationship little by little without any big argument.
The relationship didn't end with a fight; it simply stopped working over time.

questionText: Você quer aconselhar um amigo a passar mais tempo de qualidade com os filhos em vez de trabalhar tanto. Qual frase em inglês expressa esse conselho de forma mais natural e persuasiva?
correctAnswer: The kids grow up so fast. Don't let work take over — you'll never get these years back.
options: The kids grow up so fast. Don't let work take over — you'll never get these years back.
You should spend more time with your children because work is not everything.
Your children need your presence, so you should reduce your working hours.
It is important to prioritize your family time over your professional activities.

questionText: Você quer descrever que dois parentes reataram o relacionamento após anos de estrangulamento. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: After years of estrangement, they finally buried the hatchet and reconciled.
options: After years of estrangement, they finally buried the hatchet and reconciled.
After being separated for many years, they forgave each other and reconnected.
They stopped fighting after years apart and decided to have a relationship again.
Following years of distance, they resolved their differences and became close again.

questionText: Um amigo está passando por dificuldades no casamento e pediu sua opinião. Você quer dar um conselho honesto sem ser julgamental. Qual frase em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: I'm not here to judge, but from what you've told me, couples counselling might be worth considering.
options: I'm not here to judge, but from what you've told me, couples counselling might be worth considering.
I think you and your partner should go to a therapist to solve your problems.
Based on what you said, I believe your marriage needs professional help.
Without judging, I think you should seek help from a marriage counsellor.

questionText: Você quer descrever que seus pais sempre mantiveram a tradição familiar de reunir toda a família nas festas de fim de ano. Qual frase em inglês expressa essa ideia de forma mais idiomática?
correctAnswer: My parents have always made a point of bringing the whole family together over the holidays.
options: My parents have always made a point of bringing the whole family together over the holidays.
My parents always organize a family gathering during the Christmas and New Year period.
Every year my parents gather all the family members for holiday celebrations.
My parents have a tradition of uniting the family during the festive season annually.

questionText: Você quer expressar que ficou magoado porque um amigo próximo não o convidou para um evento importante. Qual frase em inglês é a mais natural para comunicar esse sentimento de forma madura?
correctAnswer: I'll be honest — I felt a bit left out when I heard about the party. I thought we were closer than that.
options: I'll be honest — I felt a bit left out when I heard about the party. I thought we were closer than that.
I was upset because you didn't invite me to the event and I felt excluded.
It hurt me to know that you had a party and I was not included in the invitation.
I felt sad when I found out about the event because you didn't think of me.

questionText: Você quer descrever que seu parceiro e você têm um relacionamento muito transparente, onde não há segredos entre vocês. Qual frase idiomática em inglês captura essa ideia?
correctAnswer: We have an open book kind of relationship — there are no secrets between us.
options: We have an open book kind of relationship — there are no secrets between us.
My partner and I are very transparent and we don't hide anything from each other.
We have a very honest relationship where we always tell each other everything.
There is complete honesty in our relationship and we share all information.

questionText: Você quer expressar que seu parceiro é o seu suporte emocional mais importante, especialmente em momentos de crise. Qual expressão em inglês é a mais idiomática?
correctAnswer: He's my rock — I honestly don't know how I'd get through life without him.
options: He's my rock — I honestly don't know how I'd get through life without him.
My partner is very important for my emotional stability and I need him a lot.
He gives me great support in difficult moments and I am grateful for that.
Without my partner, I would have a harder time dealing with difficult situations.

questionText: Você quer dizer diplomaticamente a um amigo que ele precisa estabelecer limites com seus pais superprotetores, sem parecer crítico. Qual frase em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: It might be worth having a gentle but honest conversation with them about giving you a bit more space.
options: It might be worth having a gentle but honest conversation with them about giving you a bit more space.
You need to tell your parents to stop controlling your life and give you freedom.
I think you should speak to your parents about their overprotective behaviour.
You must set limits with your parents because they interfere too much in your affairs.

questionText: Você quer descrever que uma pessoa carregou sozinha o peso de cuidar dos pais idosos por muitos anos, sem apoio dos irmãos. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: She bore the brunt of caring for their ageing parents — her siblings barely lifted a finger.
options: She bore the brunt of caring for their ageing parents — her siblings barely lifted a finger.
She took care of the old parents alone while her brothers and sisters did nothing.
The entire responsibility of caring for the elderly parents fell solely on her shoulders.
She was responsible for the parents by herself because her siblings refused to help.

questionText: Você quer expressar de forma sofisticada que a dinâmica familiar de alguém é repleta de tensões não resolvidas que afetam todos os membros. Qual frase em inglês captura esse conceito de forma mais precisa e sofisticada?
correctAnswer: There's a great deal of unresolved tension simmering beneath the surface in that family.
options: There's a great deal of unresolved tension simmering beneath the surface in that family.
That family has many problems that are not resolved and this affects everyone.
The members of that family have lots of tension that they haven't dealt with.
There are unresolved conflicts in that family that create a difficult atmosphere.

questionText: Você quer descrever que duas pessoas em um relacionamento têm uma comunicação muito eficiente e se entendem sem precisar de muitas palavras. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês é a mais precisa?
correctAnswer: They have an almost telepathic understanding — they can finish each other's sentences.
options: They have an almost telepathic understanding — they can finish each other's sentences.
Those two communicate very well and they understand each other very easily.
They know each other so well that they rarely need to explain their thoughts.
Their communication is so good that they understand what the other is thinking.

questionText: Você está descrevendo a complexidade emocional de alguém que foi adotado e está tentando reconciliar a identidade com sua família biológica e adotiva ao mesmo tempo. Qual frase em inglês articula essa nuance de forma mais sofisticada?
correctAnswer: He's navigating the complex interplay between his adoptive family's legacy and his burgeoning sense of biological identity.
options: He's navigating the complex interplay between his adoptive family's legacy and his burgeoning sense of biological identity.
He has two families and he is trying to understand who he really is.
As an adopted person, he is confused about his identity and his two families.
He faces challenges in reconciling his adoption with his biological origins.

questionText: Você quer descrever, de forma psicologicamente precisa, como padrões disfuncionais de relacionamento tendem a se repetir de geração em geração dentro de uma família. Qual frase em inglês é a mais sofisticada e precisa?
correctAnswer: Dysfunctional relational patterns have a way of perpetuating themselves across generations through unconscious mimicry.
options: Dysfunctional relational patterns have a way of perpetuating themselves across generations through unconscious mimicry.
Bad behaviours in families tend to be repeated from parents to children over many generations.
Family problems are often passed down from generation to generation without people noticing.
Unhealthy patterns of behaviour in families are frequently transmitted to the next generation.

questionText: Você quer articular, com precisão nativa, a ideia de que o estrangulamento de um membro da família foi, paradoxalmente, um ato de autopreservação psicológica e não de rejeição. Qual frase em inglês captura melhor essa nuance?
correctAnswer: What appeared to be abandonment was, in fact, a necessary act of self-preservation in the face of toxic family dynamics.
options: What appeared to be abandonment was, in fact, a necessary act of self-preservation in the face of toxic family dynamics.
He didn't abandon his family; he just needed to protect himself from their negative behaviours.
The estrangement was not rejection but rather a way to protect his mental health.
His decision to distance himself from the family was about protecting himself, not rejecting them.

questionText: Você quer descrever, com vocabulário nativo e preciso, a tensão que surge quando um filho adulto começa a questionar os valores e crenças com que foi criado, criando um distanciamento ideológico dos pais. Qual frase em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: His ideological divergence from the values instilled in him during childhood has created an almost irreconcilable chasm with his parents.
options: His ideological divergence from the values instilled in him during childhood has created an almost irreconcilable chasm with his parents.
Because he now has different beliefs from his parents, there is a big distance between them.
His new values and ideas are very different from his parents' and this causes conflict.
He no longer agrees with the values of his upbringing, which has created tension with his parents.

questionText: Você quer articular que uma mãe superprotetora, ao tentar blindar o filho de todo sofrimento, inadvertidamente comprometeu o desenvolvimento da resiliência dele. Qual frase em inglês captura essa nuance psicológica mais precisamente?
correctAnswer: By shielding him from every adversity, she unwittingly stunted his capacity for resilience.
options: By shielding him from every adversity, she unwittingly stunted his capacity for resilience.
Her excessive protection of him prevented him from learning how to deal with difficulties.
Because she protected him too much, he was unable to develop the ability to face challenges.
Her overprotective behaviour made it difficult for him to build resilience in his adult life.

questionText: Você quer descrever, com precisão nativa, o fenômeno em que um membro da família se torna o depositário das expectativas e projeções não realizadas dos pais. Qual frase em inglês articula melhor esse conceito?
correctAnswer: He became the unwitting vessel for all the unfulfilled aspirations his parents had never managed to realise for themselves.
options: He became the unwitting vessel for all the unfulfilled aspirations his parents had never managed to realise for themselves.
His parents projected their own unrealised dreams onto him, creating a burden he never asked for.
His parents transferred their own failed ambitions to him without his awareness or consent.
He was made to carry the weight of his parents' unachieved goals throughout his upbringing.

questionText: Em uma conversa sofisticada sobre relacionamentos modernos, você quer articular que dois parceiros podem manter sua individualidade sem comprometer a profundidade da intimidade compartilhada. Qual frase em inglês é a mais precisa e nativa?
correctAnswer: True intimacy doesn't demand the dissolution of selfhood; two people can remain distinct individuals while forging a profound bond.
options: True intimacy doesn't demand the dissolution of selfhood; two people can remain distinct individuals while forging a profound bond.
People in relationships can maintain their individual identity while still being very close to each other.
A couple can have a deep connection and still be independent from each other in their personal lives.
It is possible to be in a loving relationship without losing your own personality and individual identity.

questionText: Você quer descrever, com precisão psicológica e vocabulário nativo, a dinâmica em que um cônjuge sistematicamente invalida as percepções do outro, fazendo-o duvidar da própria realidade. Qual expressão idiomática em inglês é a mais precisa para esse fenômeno?
correctAnswer: He engaged in a sustained pattern of gaslighting, methodically eroding her trust in her own perceptions.
options: He engaged in a sustained pattern of gaslighting, methodically eroding her trust in her own perceptions.
He was constantly manipulating her by denying her reality and making her doubt herself.
His behaviour involved systematically invalidating her feelings to make her question her judgment.
He deliberately distorted her perception of reality by repeatedly denying her experiences.

questionText: Você quer articular, com sofisticação nativa, que a incapacidade de um casal de comunicar vulnerabilidade emocional foi, em última análise, o que levou ao colapso do relacionamento. Qual frase em inglês expressa isso de forma mais precisa e idiomática?
correctAnswer: Their mutual inability to be emotionally vulnerable with one another ultimately proved to be the undoing of their relationship.
options: Their mutual inability to be emotionally vulnerable with one another ultimately proved to be the undoing of their relationship.
The fact that they couldn't show their emotions to each other eventually destroyed the relationship.
Their unwillingness to be open about their feelings was the main reason the relationship failed.
Because neither of them could express vulnerability, the relationship could not survive in the long term.

questionText: Você quer descrever, com precisão nativa, o processo pelo qual uma pessoa reconstrói sua identidade após o término de um relacionamento longo e significativo que definiu quem ela era. Qual frase em inglês captura esse conceito mais sofisticadamente?
correctAnswer: Disentangling one's sense of self from a long-term partnership is a profoundly disorienting yet ultimately liberating process.
options: Disentangling one's sense of self from a long-term partnership is a profoundly disorienting yet ultimately liberating process.
Rebuilding your identity after a long relationship ends is very difficult but can be a positive experience.
After a long relationship finishes, a person must rediscover who they are outside of the partnership.
It is challenging but rewarding to find your own identity again after a long relationship ends.

questionText: Você quer articular, de forma academicamente precisa, como o conceito de apego ansioso desenvolvido na infância influencia os padrões relacionais na vida adulta. Qual frase em inglês é a mais sofisticada e nativa?
correctAnswer: Anxious attachment styles forged in early childhood have a profound tendency to manifest as clinginess or emotional volatility in adult romantic relationships.
options: Anxious attachment styles forged in early childhood have a profound tendency to manifest as clinginess or emotional volatility in adult romantic relationships.
People with anxious attachment from childhood often have problems with insecurity in their adult relationships.
Childhood attachment issues tend to create relationship difficulties such as excessive dependence in adults.
Insecure attachment developed in childhood frequently leads to emotional problems in adult partnerships.

questionText: Você quer descrever, com vocabulário nativo e sofisticado, o fenômeno em que membros adultos de uma família nunca confrontam diretamente os problemas, preferindo abordagens indiretas e evasivas que perpetuam o conflito latente. Qual frase em inglês captura esse fenômeno mais precisamente?
correctAnswer: That family operates by an unspoken code of conflict avoidance, tiptoeing around grievances that have festered for decades.
options: That family operates by an unspoken code of conflict avoidance, tiptoeing around grievances that have festered for decades.
The members of that family avoid confrontation and never speak directly about their long-standing problems.
That family never deals with their conflicts openly and their unresolved issues have grown over the years.
They have an unwritten rule against confrontation, leaving old resentments unaddressed for many years.

questionText: Você quer articular, com precisão nativa, a ambivalência emocional de alguém que ama profundamente um familiar mas reconhece que a proximidade desse relacionamento é emocionalmente prejudicial para si. Qual frase em inglês captura essa nuance com maior sofisticação?
correctAnswer: She loves him unconditionally, yet has come to accept that maintaining any proximity to him exacts too high an emotional toll.
options: She loves him unconditionally, yet has come to accept that maintaining any proximity to him exacts too high an emotional toll.
She loves him very much but has realised that being close to him is emotionally damaging for her.
Despite her deep love for him, she has acknowledged that their relationship is too harmful to maintain.
She cares for him deeply but understands that staying close to him is not good for her wellbeing.

questionText: Você quer descrever, com precisão e vocabulário nativo de nível C2, como a ausência emocional de um pai, embora presente fisicamente, pode criar um tipo de luto ambíguo nos filhos. Qual frase em inglês é a mais adequada?
correctAnswer: His physical presence coupled with his emotional unavailability left his children mourning a father who was, in every meaningful sense, already absent.
options: His physical presence coupled with his emotional unavailability left his children mourning a father who was, in every meaningful sense, already absent.
Although he was physically there, his emotional distance made his children feel as though they had no father.
He was present at home but emotionally absent, which caused his children to grieve for a father they never truly had.
Despite living with them, his emotional coldness meant his children experienced a form of fatherlessness.

questionText: Você quer articular, com sofisticação nativa, que o ato de perdoar um familiar que causou profundo dano não implica necessariamente a reconciliação ou a restauração do relacionamento. Qual frase em inglês articula essa distinção de forma mais precisa?
correctAnswer: Forgiveness, in this context, is less about reconciliation than about releasing oneself from the corrosive weight of resentment.
options: Forgiveness, in this context, is less about reconciliation than about releasing oneself from the corrosive weight of resentment.
Forgiving a family member doesn't mean you have to restore the relationship or be close to them again.
You can forgive someone in your family without necessarily choosing to reconcile or reconnect with them.
Forgiveness is about freeing yourself from negative feelings, not about repairing the damaged relationship.

questionText: Você quer descrever, com precisão nativa e linguagem sofisticada, como o papel de 'peacekeeper' dentro de uma família disfuncional pode ser, ao mesmo tempo, um mecanismo de controle emocional e uma fonte de esgotamento para quem o desempenha. Qual frase em inglês articula isso mais precisamente?
correctAnswer: The family peacekeeper, while ostensibly a stabilising force, often bears a disproportionate emotional burden that gradually depletes their own wellbeing.
options: The family peacekeeper, while ostensibly a stabilising force, often bears a disproportionate emotional burden that gradually depletes their own wellbeing.
The person who keeps peace in a dysfunctional family often becomes emotionally exhausted from the responsibility.
Being the peacekeeper in a troubled family is a very tiring role that can have negative effects on the person.
The family member who tries to resolve conflicts often ends up carrying too much emotional weight themselves.
